account_schoolVerificationDesc: Verify with your NTNU / NTU / NTUST email to set your username to your student ID.
account_verificationSent: Verification email sent! Check your inbox and click the link.
admin_courseActionsSubtitle: Operate the course without hand-written API calls
admin_deleteProblemMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this problem? This action cannot be undone. All testcases, submissions, and related data will be permanently removed.
admin_publishTooltip: Requires: basic info saved and at least one testcase set
admin_convertToAdvancedHint: Need a custom grading environment or a more complex workflow? You can convert this problem to Advanced Mode.
admin_convertToAdvancedInlineWarning: ⚠️ This will DELETE all workspace files, testcases, and judge settings — it cannot be undone.
admin_convertToAdvancedDesc: Advanced Mode gives you full control over the judge container, testcase flow, and judge script — ideal for custom grading needs.
admin_convertToAdvancedWarningHeader: ⚠️ Warning: this action cannot be undone
admin_convertToAdvancedWarningItem1: All workspace files will be deleted
admin_convertToAdvancedWarningItem2: All testcase sets and testcases will be deleted
admin_convertToAdvancedWarningItem3: Judge config and scoring rules will be reset
admin_inputFormatTooltip: Describe the input format. Supports Markdown.
admin_outputFormatTooltip: Describe the output format. Supports Markdown.
admin_publishConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to publish this problem? It will be visible to students after publishing.
admin_statementTooltip: Supports Markdown syntax. Describe the problem, constraints, and examples.
admin_summaryTooltip: A short description shown in the problem list. If empty, the beginning of the statement will be used.
admin_tagsPlaceholder: Type and press space to add tags
admin_unsavedChangesMessage: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?
admin_helpVisibility:
Public: visible in problem list, anyone can submit.
Private: only the author, admins, and course members with access can view and submit.
admin_helpJudgeType:
Standard: compare output exactly.
Checker: use a custom script to validate output.
Interactive: the judge communicates with the submission via stdin/stdout.
admin_helpCheckerScript: The checker receives 3 file paths as arguments: sys.argv[1] = input data, sys.argv[2] = expected output, sys.argv[3] = contestant output. Print AC/WA and exit with code 0/1.
admin_helpInteractorScript: The interactor communicates with the submission via stdin/stdout. Read contestant output from stdin, write test input to stdout. Remember to flush after each write.
admin_helpStatement: The problem description shown to contestants. Supports Markdown syntax.
admin_helpInputFormat: Describe the format of the input data (e.g. number of lines, variable types, constraints). Supports Markdown.
admin_helpOutputFormat: Describe the expected output format. Supports Markdown.
admin_tabDisabledTooltip: Please save basic info first to unlock this tab
admin_tabLocked: Please complete and save basic info first
admin_staticAnalysisDesc: Check code before compilation to restrict specific syntax or libraries
admin_bannedFunctionsPlaceholder: Enter function name and press space
admin_bannedImportsPlaceholder: Enter module name and press space
admin_artifactCollectionDesc: Collect files produced by student code for download and debugging
admin_networkAccessDesc: Allow student code to access external networks
admin_customScriptsDesc: Insert custom scripts at specific pipeline stages
admin_helpTimeLimitMs: Max execution time per testcase (100–30000 ms). Typical: 1000 ms for most problems, up to 5000 ms for heavy I/O.
admin_helpMemoryLimitMb: Max memory per testcase (16–1024 MB). Typical: 256 MB. Exceeding this causes Memory Limit Exceeded (MLE).
admin_helpSetWeight: Points awarded for this testcase set. Weight 0 = sample (visible to students, not scored). Weights of scored sets should sum to 100.
admin_helpSetHidden: Hidden sets are not shown to students on the problem page. Typically sample sets are visible and graded sets are hidden.
admin_helpBannedFunctions: Function names that are forbidden. Submissions using these will be rejected before execution.
admin_helpBannedImports: Module/library names that cannot be imported (e.g. os, subprocess, socket).
admin_helpBannedPatterns: Regex patterns to detect in source code. Matched code will be flagged or rejected.
admin_helpLinterCommand: Shell command to lint student code (e.g. pylint --disable=C). Runs before compilation.
admin_helpOnLintFailure: Fail: reject submission immediately. Warn: still judge but show lint warnings.
admin_helpArtifactPatterns: Glob patterns for files to collect after execution (e.g. *.bmp, output/*). Students can download these.
admin_helpMaxArtifactSize: Maximum total size of collected artifacts. Prevents excessive storage use.
admin_helpFirewallRules: Allow student code to access specific hosts/ports. All other network access is blocked.
admin_helpSidecarServices: Auxiliary containers (e.g. database, API server) available to student code during execution.
admin_helpLogTraffic: Record all network traffic for debugging and review. May impact performance.
admin_helpCustomScripts: Custom scripts that run at specific pipeline stages (before-compile, after-compile, after-check).
admin_helpScriptRunAt:
before-compile: runs before compilation.
after-compile: runs after successful compilation.
after-check: runs after all testcases are judged.
admin_helpScoringStrategy:
All-or-nothing: full points only if all testcases pass.
Proportional: partial credit (e.g. 3/5 pass = 60%).
Minimum: score = worst testcase result.
admin_helpLatePenaltyFixed: Deduct a fixed amount per time period after the deadline. Capped at max deduction.
admin_helpLatePenaltyDecay: Score decays exponentially. Half-life = time until score is halved.
admin_helpTimeBonus: Bonus points for fast execution. Max bonus at 0 ms, linearly decreasing to 0 at baseline.
admin_helpMemoryPenalty: Deduct points if memory usage exceeds the threshold.
admin_helpCustomScoringScript:
Script receives a JSON payload on stdin with rawScore, testcaseResults, submittedAt, runtimeMs, and more.
Must output JSON with finalScore and feedback fields.
admin_helpZipCompileCommand: Command to compile the project (e.g. make, gcc main.c -o main). Leave empty for interpreted languages.
admin_helpZipEntryPoint: Main file to execute (e.g. main.c, src/main.py). Used to determine the run command.
admin_subtaskScoringStrategyDesc: Configure scoring method for each subtask
admin_noSubtasksHint: Please add testcase sets in the Testcase Management tab first
admin_scoreAdjustmentRulesDesc: Adjust final score based on submission time or resource usage
admin_customScoringScriptDesc: Use a script for custom scoring logic
admin_scriptOverrideWarning: Enabling this will override the rules above
assessment_assignmentExplanation: Assignments emphasize deadlines, progress, and course context. They still submit through the judge, but the UI avoids contest-rank pressure.

assignmentsTop_emptyDescription: Once you're added to a course, every assignment will show up here.
assignmentsTop_subtitle: Every assignment from every course you're in. To create, edit, or configure, go into the course.

auth_noAccount: Don't have an account? Sign up

courseSettings_visibilityPlaceholder: Archive toggle will be added once the course archivedAt column lands. Until then, use assignment-level open/close windows to control student access.

courseSettings_deleteDesc: Includes all assignments, exams, student submissions, members, announcements, scoreboards, and plagiarism reports. This action cannot be undone.

courseSettings_deleteMismatchError: The course name you typed does not match
courseSettings_copyUnavailable: Copy course is not available yet
examsTop_canAnswer: You can answer once it starts
examsTop_emptyDescription: When your courses publish exams they will show up here.

examsTop_subtitle: Every exam from every course you are in. Create or edit them inside the course.
latePenalty_noneDesc: Late submissions are not penalized. Use with a hard final-day cutoff.
latePenalty_flatDesc: Late submissions lose a fixed percentage of the final score, regardless of how late.
latePenalty_dailyDesc: Linear decay: lose N% per day. One day late = N%, two days late = 2N%, floored at zero.
latePenalty_finalDayZeroDesc: Submissions after the final day are scored zero. Hardest cutoff, suits exam-style work.
account_invalidSchoolEmail: Please enter a valid three-school email (ntnu / ntu / ntust)

courseJoin_hint: If the current actor does not have the right role, switch actor from the header first and retry the join action. The same actor will then be used across problems and submissions.

editor_runFirst: Run your code to see results.
hero_subtitle: Solve problems, join contests, and track your progress.
home_productDescription: Online judge platform for programming courses — solve problems, join contests, and submit assignments.
members_addDescription: Paste a list of handles (one per line, or comma-separated). The system will parse each handle and attach the matching student. Unknown handles become placeholders that auto-merge when the student first logs in with OAuth.
members_hint: No email will be sent and no join link is generated.
members_placeholderFooter: Placeholder rows are visually dimmed (? avatar, empty name) so you can see at a glance who hasn't signed in yet.
members_roleTaDesc: Can edit assignments, view class stats.
onboarding_usernamePatternError: Use 3-64 lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, or underscores.
onboarding_usernamePatternHint: 3-64 characters, lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, underscores
onboarding_usernameReserved: This username format is reserved for school accounts.
onboarding_schoolOptionDesc: Verify with your NTNU / NTU / NTUST email
onboarding_subtitle: Choose how to set your username
onboarding_verificationSent: Verification email sent! Check your inbox and click the link.
problemDetail_checkerHelp: Your stdout is handed to a custom checker script, which decides correctness. The expected output doesn't have to match byte-for-byte — useful for problems with multiple valid answers.
problemDetail_fullSourceHelp: Submit a complete program including main(). The judge compiles and runs your code against stdin and compares stdout against the expected output.
problemDetail_interactiveHelp: Your program talks back and forth with an interactor over stdin/stdout. Each reply from the interactor depends on your previous output — no fixed input/output file.
problemDetail_multiFileHelp: The problem ships multiple files — one or more you edit plus read-only helpers (and sometimes hidden files). You edit the designated files in the browser and submit them together.
problemDetail_specialEnvHelp: Judging runs entirely inside a teacher-provided Docker image. You upload a tarball containing your submission and the image decides how to build, run, and grade it. Because the image is the single source of truth, the usual judging-method badges (standard / checker / interactive) don't apply here.
problemDetail_standardHelp: Your stdout is compared line by line against the expected output. Trailing whitespace is normalised, but otherwise the comparison is exact.
problems_myProblemsEmpty: You haven't created any problems yet.
runtimeStats_loading: Waiting for the runtime counters to load.
submissions_signInRequired: Please sign in to view submissions.
testcases_hiddenCasesHint: Used for grading only. Not visible to students.
testcases_sampleCasesHint: Shown on the problem page as examples.
testcases_uploadZipHint: Upload a ZIP file containing testcases
testcases_zipFormatHelp:
ZIP structure: each testcase is a pair of files sharing the same base name.
Example: 1-01.in / 1-01.out, 1-02.in / 1-02.out, 2-01.in / 2-01.out …
The first capture group in the regex identifies the subtask, the second identifies the case.
Supported: up to 20 subtasks, 256 cases per subtask, 1 MB per file. Max ZIP size: 50 MB.
testcases_zipStructureHint:
Regex with 2 capture groups:
(1) subtask ID, (2) case ID.
Default (\d+)-(\d+) matches 1-01.in.
For fixed-width digits, e.g. (\d\d)(\d\d) matches 0103.in.

workspace_descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this file is for…

coursesNew_placeholderInfoBody: There is no invite link, no join code, no public sign-up. If a student hasn't registered yet, the system creates a placeholder; the first time they sign in via OAuth it will auto-attach and the course will appear for them. Manage members from the “Members” tab after the course is created.
coursesNew_actionsHint: Live as soon as it's created. Only the students you add will see it.

admin_announcementsSubtitle: Keep users informed with pinned notices and platform updates.

problems_createAdvancedDescription: TA provides a Docker image that handles grading
advancedMode_uploadInstructions: This problem uses Advanced Mode. Upload a ZIP archive or a single source file. Grading runs in a container provided by the TA.
admin_standardNormalizationHint: Standard OJ normalization is applied to outputs: CRLF is converted to LF, trailing whitespace is stripped per line, and trailing blank lines are ignored. For float tolerance or any custom comparison, switch to a Checker.
admin_checkerHelpBody: The sandbox calls your script with three files: the testcase input, the expected output, and the student output. Python: `judge_input`, `judge_output`, and `process_output` are pre-bound as strings, and helper functions `accept(feedback="")`, `reject(feedback="")`, and `partial(score, feedback="")` exit with the right status. C++: include `testlib.h` and use `registerTestlibCmd`, then `inf` / `ouf` / `ans` for reading and `quitf(_ok, ...)` / `quitf(_wa, ...)` / `quitp(score, ...)` for finishing. Feedback is shown to the student. Checker timeout is 30 seconds; a crash or hang becomes a system error and does not count against the student.
admin_interactorHelpBody: The interactor and student program are connected with a bidirectional pipe: student stdout flows to interactor stdin, and interactor stdout flows to student stdin. Python: `judge_input` is the testcase input string, `read()` reads one line from the student (auto-rejecting if the stream closes), and `write(msg)` sends one line back (already flushed). Helpers `accept(feedback="", score=100)`, `reject(feedback="", score=0)`, and `partial(score, feedback="")` finish the run; the score goes to stderr and feedback is shown to the student. C++: use `registerInteraction(argc, argv)` plus `cout` / `cin`, and remember to `cout << flush` before reading. The student and interactor share the problem's time limit.
admin_allowedLanguagesHint: Tick the languages students may submit in.
admin_noFilesForLang: No files for {language}. Click “+ Add” to create one.
admin_workspaceModeHint: Pick how students will submit their code. You can switch later, but review your files before doing so.
admin_workspaceModeFullSourceDesc: Students submit one complete source file (including main()). The starter template below is optional — it just gives students a starting point.
admin_workspaceModeMultiFileDesc: You ship a full file tree (main + helpers); students can only edit files marked editable. Every allowed language must ship a main file.
admin_workspaceFilesHintFullSource: Optionally provide starter code per language. Students see this template when they open the editor. Leave blank for an empty editor.
admin_workspaceFilesHintMultiFile: Provide one editable main file per allowed language, plus any helper files (readonly or hidden).
admin_workspaceMissingTemplatesBanner: These languages still need a main file: {languages}
admin_workspaceMissingTemplatesPrompt: Switch to each language below and click “Create main file”.
admin_workspaceNoLanguagesSelected: Tick at least one language in “Allowed languages” above.
admin_workspaceEmptyFullSource: No starter template for {language}. Leaving this blank is fine for single-file problems — students will see an empty editor.
admin_workspaceEmptyMultiFile: No files for {language} yet. Multi-file problems need at least a main file named {filename}.
admin_sampleIOHint: Samples are shown to students on the problem page. They do not count toward grading.
admin_noSamplesYet: No samples yet. Add one to show students an example I/O pair.
testcases_zipTooLarge: ZIP file too large ({size} MB). Maximum is 50 MB.
testcases_scoringStrategyHint: All-or-nothing: any failing testcase makes the subtask score 0. Proportional: subtask score = (passing cases / total) × weight. Minimum: subtask score = min(per-case scores) × weight / 100, useful when a checker reports partial scores.
admin_judgeImageHint: Provide a Docker image. The container must bundle its own testcases and grading logic.
admin_imageRefHint: e.g. {example} — the worker will pull this at grading time.
admin_tarballDropHint: Drop a docker tarball here, or click to browse
admin_totalTimeLimitHint: Total budget for the entire container (not per-testcase).
admin_containerContractHint: The TA’s image is fully self-contained: testcases and grading scripts must be bundled into the image. The system only passes in student files and reads the result you write.
admin_noNetwork: Container runs with {flag}, no network access.
admin_exitCodeWarning: Non-zero exit code → runtime_error; missing {file} or invalid format → SE.
